refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safety-critical teleoperation, focusing on communication latency as the key functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data and validate p95 latency ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time feedback to operator to maintain safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and compression algorithms to reduce latency.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to process data closer to the vehicle.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over other data streams via QoS mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and compression algorithms to reduce latency.

potential_sources: Interaction between network protocol optimization and vehicle control data streams
Dependency on network link quality and telemetry data accuracy
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses the latency requirement with minimal coupling to other system functions; edge computing and QoS are complementary but not selected here.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed baseline for latency measurement.
Risk of protocol incompatibility with existing vehicle communication systems.
Risk that compression algorithms may introduce processing delays.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP2) if latency targets are not met in testing.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for implementing teleoperation communication protocols, latency optimization, and operator interface data handling.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional communication channel between Teleoperation module and Vehicle module for control commands and telemetry.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Incoming telemetry data from Vehicle to Teleoperation for latency monitoring and feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Control commands sent from Teleoperation to Vehicle.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope; low containment specificity for latency optimization.
Vehicle handles control execution but not latency optimization logic.
Primary module for latency optimization and teleoperation communication.
Safety Services monitors alerts but does not implement latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency optimization logic and communication protocol handling are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and maximize ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential cross-module dependencies with Vehicle for telemetry and control command timing.
Risk of interface mismatches causing latency spikes if Network Link interface is unstable.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define strict interface contracts and latency monitoring metrics between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear latency reduction strategies and measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if latency optimization logic spreads into Vehicle or NGHE Solution modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows high containment score and low diffusion, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry and network link monitoring within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-related functions to Teleoperation module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Strengthen interface definitions between Teleoperation and Vehicle to reduce latency spikes.
Plan for fallback or escalation to edge computing if latency targets are not met.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignment meets the requirements with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Verification and ownership are well defined with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time feedback to operator to maintain safe remote operation.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Telemetry data is accurate and timely.
open_risks: Protocol incompatibility and processing delays from compression algorithms.
Interface stability between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under operational conditions.
Interface performance metrics between Teleoperation and Vehicle.
